def test_refund_question_retrieves_policy_chunk():
        """
        Run the CLI with a refund-related question and verify the retrieved
        context chunk content is printed to stdout (case-insensitive match on
        a substring drawn from the ingested policy document).
        """
        proc = _run_cli("What is your refund policy?")
>       assert proc.returncode == 0, (

{proc.stdout}
stderr:
{proc.stderr}"
        )

    "expected": "object",

    ],
    "message": "Invalid input: expected object, received undefined"

    "code": "invalid_type",
    "path": [

    ],
    "message": "Invalid input: expected record, received undefined"
  }
]', 'status': 400}

tests.name: test_final_state.py::test_second_invocation_does_not_409
tests.trace:
def test_second_invocation_does_not_409():
        """
        Running the CLI a second time with the same arguments must not crash
        with an uncaught 409 Conflict. The second invocation must still
        produce a retrieval whose stdout contains the policy chunk.
        """
        proc = _run_cli("What is your refund policy?")
>       assert proc.returncode == 0, (
            "Second invocation of the CLI failed. The CLI must remain idempotent "
            "across reruns (e.g., by using a unique file_name suffix from "
            "ZEALT_RUN_ID, by handling 409 Conflict, or by delete-then-add).\n"
            f"stdout:\n{proc.stdout}\nstderr:\n{proc.stderr}"
        )
E       AssertionError: Second invocation of the CLI failed. The CLI must remain idempotent across reruns (e.g., by using a unique file_name suffix from ZEALT_RUN_ID, by handling 409 Conflict, or by delete-then-add).
E         stdout:
E         
E         stderr:
E         Error adding document: Error code: 400 - {'statusText': 'BAD_REQUEST', 'success': False, 'message': 'Fields missing. Detailed error: [\n  {\n    "expected": "object",\n    "code": "invalid_type",\n    "path": [\n      "metadata"\n    ],\n    "message": "Invalid input: expected object, received undefined"\n  },\n  {\n    "expected": "record",\n    "code": "invalid_type",\n    "path": [\n      "metadata"\n    ],\n    "message": "Invalid input: expected record, received undefined"\n  }\n]', 'status': 400}
E         
E       assert 1 == 0
E        +  where 1 = CompletedProcess(args=['python3', 'main.py', '--question', 'What is your refund policy?'], returncode=1, stdout='', st...adata"\\n    ],\\n    "message": "Invalid input: expected record, received undefined"\\n  }\\n]\', \'status\': 400}\n').returncode
